title: Create a house-standard Angular application
description: Scaffold a BeSpunky-standard Angular app — the application sibling of publishable-lib. Delegates to @nx/angular:application with the house defaults (minimal, scss, routing, no e2e), then applies the per-app house config the raw generator omits: serve host 0.0.0.0 (reachable from outside the devcontainer) via the serve-options generator, and — when the workspace is a Firebase workspace — the full per-app Firebase wiring (environment files, firebase.config.ts, app.config wiring, production fileReplacements, platform:web tag, serve orchestrator) via the firebase-emulators generator. Firebase is self-detecting: an explicit --firebase wins (the scaffolder's first app, created before firebase.json exists), otherwise it is inferred from firebase.json at the workspace root — so a second app added to a Firebase workspace is configured identically to the first with no flag. This is the single command for adding any further app to a BeSpunky workspace; never reach for the raw @nx/angular:application generator, which leaves the per-app config out.

directory.description: Workspace-relative directory for the app, e.g. `apps/<name>`. The project name defaults to the directory's last segment.
directory.x-prompt: Where should the app live (e.g. apps/<name>)?

name.description: Explicit project name. Defaults to the directory's last segment (matching @nx/angular).

firebase.description: Apply the per-app Firebase wiring. When omitted, auto-detected from the presence of firebase.json at the workspace root (so apps added to a Firebase workspace get wired automatically). Pass explicitly only to force it on a workspace that doesn't yet have firebase.json (the scaffolder's first app) or to override detection.

style.description: The file extension or preprocessor to use for component styles.

workspaceName.description: Override the workspace identity that seeds the emulators' offline `demo-<workspaceName>` project id. Defaults to the workspace root directory name — correct in every normal case; do not pass it unless retargeting.
